recommendations.title: Create <DitaSteps> component for procedural content
recommendations.description: Wrap numbered procedures in <DitaSteps> to restore <steps>/<step>/<cmd>/<info>/<stepresult> hierarchy

recommendations.title: Convert note/warning/caution text to Docusaurus admonitions
Remove duplicated list/step content
recommendations.description: Delete plain-text copies of content that already appears in properly formatted lists

recommendations.title: Create <Prereq> and <TaskResult> components
recommendations.description: Restore DITA prereq/postreq/result sections with semantic components

recommendations.title: Create <UIControl> and <MenuCascade> components
recommendations.description: Recover <uicontrol> and <menucascade> semantics for UI element references

recommendations.title: Recover missing image references
recommendations.description: Locate original DITA image files and add proper or tags

recommendations.title: Rejoin remaining fragmented sentences
recommendations.description: Manually rejoin the ~43 remaining dangling preposition fragments in complex structures (tables, multi-clause sentences)

recommendations.title: Reconstruct remaining truncated step stubs
recommendations.description: Manually fix step stubs that could not be auto-matched to their full-text counterparts
recommendations.automated: Majority resolved by PASS-03; edge cases remain

recommendations.title: Reconstruct broken Markdown tables from plain-text table remnants
recommendations.description: DITA <simpletable>/<table>/<properties> elements were converted to plain text with header words on standalone lines and data as paragraphs. Manually reconstruct as proper Markdown pipe-delimited tables.
recommendations.automated: 96% of broken tables reconstructed by PASS-04; 2 files with residual issues remain

cleanupHistory.description: Automated cleanup passes applied to remediate DITA conversion artifacts

passes.name: Duplicate Content Removal
passes.description: Removed plain-text copies of content that already appeared in properly formatted bullet/numbered lists. The DITA converter emitted list item text at both the <li> container level and as child text nodes, causing every list item to appear twice.
passes.rootCause:
DITA
    /
      / elements have nested child elements (

      , , ) that the converter extracted separately from the parent

    1. / container.

passes.name: Fragmented Sentence Rejoining
passes.description: Stitched back together sentences broken by DITA <uicontrol> tag stripping. The converter emitted each <uicontrol> value on its own line instead of keeping it inline, fragmenting fluid sentences into 3-10 separate lines.
passes.rootCause: DITA inline elements (<uicontrol>, <wintitle>, <codeph>) were stripped of tags but their content was emitted as standalone lines rather than kept inline within the parent sentence.
passes.technique: Bold-wrapped rejoined UI terms (**Admin Settings**) to preserve <uicontrol> semantic distinction

passes.name: Truncated Step Reconstruction
passes.description: Reconstructed numbered/bullet list items that were truncated at <uicontrol> tag boundaries. The converter emitted step stubs (e.g., "1. Select the") with the full step text as plain paragraphs below. Matched each stub to its full-text counterpart by prefix matching and merged them.
passes.rootCause: DITA <step>/<cmd> content was split at inline <uicontrol> tags: the text before the tag became a truncated numbered item, the full text (with tag content) was emitted separately below.

passes.name: Broken Table Reconstruction
passes.description: Reconstructed DITA <simpletable>/<table>/<properties> content that was converted to plain text back into proper Markdown pipe-delimited tables. Detected consecutive header words (e.g., "Setting/Description") followed by alternating key/description blocks and rebuilt them as Markdown tables.
passes.rootCause: DITA table elements lost all pipe-delimited structure during conversion. Table headers and cell data appeared as plain text on separate lines.
passes.technique: Pattern-matched consecutive header words, parsed key/description blocks, rebuilt as Markdown pipe-delimited tables. Manually corrected 4-column tables and Problem/Solution tables.

remainingIssues.description: Issues remaining after all automated cleanup passes

categories.note: Mostly inside table cells or multi-clause sentences where auto-rejoin risked corrupting content
Primarily table headers/content misdetected as fragments, code blocks, and edge-case stubs with no prefix match
Steps where the full-text version had already been partially rejoined, breaking the prefix match
DITA <simpletable>, <table>, and <properties> elements lost all pipe-delimited structure during conversion; headers and data appear as plain text lines

brandAlignmentFixes.description: Post-migration brand alignment corrections applied to resolve non-DITA cosmetic and structural issues.

fixes.label: Footer copyright
fixes.before: My Project, Inc. Built with Docusaurus.
fixes.after: Zebra Technologies Corp. All rights reserved.
fixes.reason: Replace placeholder with correct legal entity

fixes.label: Test artifact removed
fixes.reason: Placeholder file not part of product documentation

sidebarRecursiveScanning.description: Replaced flat one-level sidebar generation with recursive buildSidebarItems() function to support arbitrary nesting depth in User Guide, matching the DITA map hierarchy from docs.zebra.com.

changes.label: Recursive buildSidebarItems()
changes.before: getDocFileInfo() + single-level readdirSync — sub-subdirectories silently ignored
changes.after: Single recursive function walks any depth, auto-generating nested collapsible categories
changes.reason: DITA-to-MDX conversion preserves topic nesting that exceeds one level

changes.label: User Guide section ordering preserved
changes.detail: userGuideSectionOrder array passed as orderHint to top-level call; child levels sort alphabetically
changes.reason: Maintain docs.zebra.com navigation order at the top level

impact.buildTime: negligible — synchronous fs reads on local directory tree
impact.risk: low — only affects sidebar generation, no content changes

dashboardV2Consolidation.description: Eliminated the standalone Dashboard V2 Metrics Overview section. All 7 V2 tiles integrated into existing V1 dashboard sections, removing redundancy while preserving unique metrics.

changes.label: V2 section removed
changes.detail: Removed the entire 'Dashboard V2 Metrics Overview' block (header, global strip, tile grid)
changes.reason: 3 of 7 tiles duplicated V1 panels; separate section caused confusion

changes.label: Global stability strip relocated
changes.after: Pinned directly below Build Overview hero

changes.label: Platform Stability + Operational Health → Quality Gates
changes.detail: Unique V2 tiles (Monaco stability, PDF pipeline, snapshot freshness) added to Quality Gates section
changes.reason: Runtime stability complements build stability metrics

changes.label: Release Intelligence + JIRA Intelligence → Release & Operations
changes.detail: V2 tiles with release readiness index and JIRA traceability added alongside Epic Release Gate
changes.reason: Natural grouping with existing release metrics

changes.label: Content Intelligence → Content Health
changes.detail: V2 tile with metadata quality score and required field debt placed alongside Content Quality
changes.reason: Aggregated view of scattered V1 completeness metrics

changes.label: Search Insights + User Behavior → Platform Intelligence
changes.detail: V2 aggregated tiles placed next to their V1 detailed counterparts

changes.label: Persona presets updated
changes.detail: ALL_V1_PANELS and V1_PRESETS expanded with platformStability, operationalHealth, releaseIntelligence, jiraIntelligence; V2_PRESETS and V2_GROUP_ORDER removed
changes.reason: Single unified persona system replaces dual V1/V2 filtering

dashboardNewPanels.description: Added 6 new dashboard panels filling identified coverage gaps. Each panel placed in its semantically correct section with persona presets updated.

panels.name: i18nCoverage
panels.dataSource: report.aggregate.sections (de, fr, ja counts vs main docs)
